executive_summary: Store delivered weekly sales of 367,581.78 inc VAT (338,366.78 ex VAT) for the week ending 13 June 2026.
Overall achieved margin of 17.41% against a total waste charge of 8,590.86, representing a waste percentage of 2.54% of sales.

Department P&L data is critically compromised this week: purchases and opening stock figures are missing across all departments, resulting in cost of sales being calculated solely as the negative of closing stock. This renders all department-level margin and profit figures unreliable and not reflective of true trading performance.

The combined department total shows a closing stock figure of 134,481.07 against total department sales of 116,153.10, producing a mathematically distorted margin of 215.78% which has no commercial validity.
Waste is elevated in Fish (1,659.91), Produce (2,289.59), Deli (1,408.03), and Provisions (986.03), which together account for 6,343.55 or approximately 73.8% of total store waste.
Scan margins where available provide the only reliable theoretical margin reference this week; these range from 11.76% on Bacon and Loose SRP to 41.17% on Deli.
Immediate priority is to reconcile and repost missing purchases and opening stock data for all departments before any P&L conclusions are drawn for this trading week.
biggest_wins: Deli recorded the strongest scan margin in the store at 41.17% against weekly sales of 8,732.62, indicating solid theoretical pricing and mix performance, subject to waste management improvement given a waste charge of 1,408.03.
Produce drove the highest department sales contribution at 39,662.70 with a scan margin of 19.16%, making it the single largest revenue department this week.
Poultry achieved sales of 12,696.55 with a scan margin of 17.20%, broadly in line with store average, representing consistent volume performance in a competitive fresh category.
Lamb posted the highest scan margin among the core butchery departments at 24.80% on sales of 1,562.31, suggesting strong pricing discipline relative to cost.
Pork delivered a scan margin of 23.19% on sales of 1,732.19, the second strongest in the butchery group and above the store overall margin of 17.41%.
biggest_risks: All department purchases and opening stock values are missing from the P&L data this week. Without these inputs, COGS cannot be calculated correctly and every department margin and profit figure is invalid. This is the most significant operational reporting risk this week and must be resolved before the P&L is finalised.

Fish waste of 1,659.91 is disproportionately high relative to Fish sales of 5,393.66, implying a waste-to-sales ratio of approximately 30.8% in that department alone, which is commercially unsustainable and requires immediate ordering and rotation review.

Produce waste of 2,289.59 against sales of 39,662.70 represents approximately 5.77% waste on sales in that department, significantly above an acceptable threshold and requiring attention to ordering volumes and shelf life management.

Deli waste of 1,408.03 against sales of 8,732.62 equates to approximately 16.1% waste on sales, indicating serious over-production or insufficient sell-through in the deli counter and hot food operation.
Provisions waste of 986.03 is notable and should be reviewed against ordering patterns and ambient stock rotation practices.

Bacon and Loose SRP shows the weakest scan margin in the store at 11.76% on sales of 1,052.06, falling materially below the store overall margin of 17.41% and requiring a pricing and product mix review.

Cigarettes closing stock of 114,751.02 against sales of 7,137.91 is extremely high and, while partly structural given the nature of tobacco stockholding, should be verified for accuracy given the data integrity issues present across all departments this week.

Bread and Cakes waste of 812.13 and Dairy waste of 434.22 are not accompanied by department-level sales or margin data in this report, making it impossible to assess their impact; these categories must be included in next week's full P&L submission.
department_callouts: Cigarettes: Sales of 7,137.91 with a scan margin of 12.87%. Closing stock of 114,751.02 is very high relative to weekly sales. Purchases and opening stock missing; reported margin of 1,707.63% is not valid. Stock accuracy and reposting required.

Beef: Sales of 13,218.63 with a scan margin of 19.57%, the strongest in the butchery group. Waste of 89.06 is relatively well controlled. Purchases and opening stock missing; reported figures not usable.
Lamb: Sales of 1,562.31 with a scan margin of 24.80%. Waste of 85.09 is low and appropriate for the volume. Purchases and opening stock missing; reported margin not valid.
Pork: Sales of 1,732.19 with a scan margin of 23.19%. Waste of 117.17 is manageable. Purchases and opening stock missing; reported margin not valid.

Bacon and Loose SRP: Sales of 1,052.06 with the lowest scan margin in the store at 11.76%. Waste of 156.27 is disproportionate relative to the sales level and warrants a review of ordering and markdown activity.
Poultry: Sales of 12,696.55 with a scan margin of 17.20%, broadly in line with store average. Waste of 291.06 should be monitored; ordering and rotation controls to be reviewed.

Fish: Sales of 5,393.66 with a scan margin of 15.30%. Waste of 1,659.91 is critically high, representing approximately 30.8% of department sales. Immediate intervention required on ordering, intake scheduling, and markdown strategy.
Produce: Sales of 39,662.70 with a scan margin of 19.16%. Waste of 2,289.59 equates to approximately 5.77% of department sales. Ordering volumes and shelf life disciplines need urgent review.

Deli: Sales of 8,732.62 with the highest scan margin in the store at 41.17%. However, waste of 1,408.03 represents approximately 16.1% of sales, significantly eroding the theoretical margin advantage. Production planning and end-of-day markdown processes need immediate attention.

Provisions: Sales of 24,964.47 with a scan margin of 19.98%, slightly above store average. Waste of 986.03 is notable and rotation and date management should be reviewed across ambient chilled provisions lines.
Bread and Cakes: No sales or margin data provided in the department P&L this week. Waste of 812.13 recorded. Full department data must be included in the next submission to allow meaningful analysis.
Dairy: No sales or margin data provided in the department P&L this week. Waste of 434.22 recorded. Full department data must be included in the next submission.
Frozen: No sales or margin data provided in the department P&L this week. Waste of 262.30 recorded. Full department data must be included in the next submission.
action_plan: Accounts and Department Managers to investigate and repost missing purchases and opening stock figures for all departments as a matter of urgency so that a corrected P&L can be produced before the week is closed off.

Fish Manager to conduct an immediate review of intake volumes, delivery frequency, and markdown procedures given waste of 1,659.91 representing approximately 30.8% of Fish sales of 5,393.66 this week.
Produce Manager to review daily ordering quantities and shelf replenishment disciplines to address waste of 2,289.59, targeting a reduction toward a maximum of 3% of Produce sales.

Deli Manager to implement tighter production planning aligned to hourly sales data, with a structured end-of-day markdown and clearance protocol to reduce waste of 1,408.03 which currently represents approximately 16.1% of Deli sales.
Provisions Manager to carry out a full date rotation check across all ambient and chilled provisions lines and review ordering quantities to bring waste of 986.03 down to an acceptable level.

Category Manager and Bacon buyer to review product mix, pricing, and promotional activity for Bacon and Loose SRP where scan margin of 11.76% is the weakest in the store and falls well below the overall store margin of 17.41%.

Store Manager to ensure that Bread and Cakes, Dairy, and Frozen departments are fully included in next week's department P&L submission with sales, purchases, opening stock, and closing stock figures so that complete store-wide analysis can be performed.
Poultry and Bacon departments to review ordering cadence and chilled stock rotation to prevent waste levels from escalating further given current charges of 291.06 and 156.27 respectively.

Cigarettes stock controller to verify the accuracy of the 114,751.02 closing stock figure and reconcile against physical count, given the data integrity issues present across all departments this week.

Store Manager to schedule a waste review meeting with all fresh and food-to-go department managers by midweek to communicate that the store waste percentage of 2.54% requires reduction, with specific targets set per department.

Operations team to implement a daily waste log review process across Fish, Produce, Deli, and Provisions so that over-ordering and waste trends are identified and acted upon within the trading week rather than retrospectively.
Finance and IT to investigate the root cause of the missing purchases and opening stock data across all departments to prevent recurrence of unusable P&L data in future weekly reporting cycles.
